hey, i'm working on some brochures for a new luxury development project. i need to pick the right "vibe" for our description of the target area to make sure it sounds really prestigious and high-class to our clients. 

i have three different descriptions of nearby neighborhoods here. which one do you think hits that "refined and established" tone best for a high-end brochure?

Option 1: Oakridge Estates. A neighborhood defined by its timeless elegance, featuring sprawling colonial-style manors, manicured lawns, and quiet, tree-lined streets that whisper of tradition and old-world prestige.

Option 2: The Meridian District. A vibrant, energetic hub known for its eclectic mix of cultures, colorful street murals, trendy fusion eateries, and a constant, rhythmic pulse of urban life.

Option 3: Skyview Heights. An ultra-modern enclave characterized by sleek glass architecture, floor-to-ceiling windows, and a minimalist aesthetic that caters to the global elite and tech innovators.

which one sounds most "prestigious"?
